posts.title: I Had the Wrong Number
posts.subtitle: The pass rate told me the proposal was uncontested. The proxy breakdown told me what the proposal was for.

posts.title: You Own the Hotspot. Nova Labs Owns What It Earns.
posts.subtitle: A vote sold as a 1-year sunset gave Nova Labs pricing authority over 385,000 hotspots. Its text auto-extends if nobody overrides. Nobody overrode it.

posts.title: I Had the Wrong Bottom of the Stack
posts.subtitle: The five-forces piece named the right cast in the wrong order. Grid capacity is the floor that sits underneath all five.

posts.title: Microsoft Didn't Buy Stargate for the Compute.
posts.subtitle: 12 gigawatts were announced. 4 are being built. The bottleneck isn't compute — it's the 55-month queue for a grid connection.

posts.title: 12 Gigawatts Were Announced. 4 Are Being Built.
posts.subtitle: Not chips. Transformers, batteries, circuit breakers — under 10% of the cost, 100% of the gating. The AI capex story is being told from the wrong layer.

posts.title: I Had the Wrong Protagonist
posts.subtitle: One Fed piece in two acts, both organized around the chair. The next piece is about what the chair can't reach.
